text.title: Pricing Research (Researcher) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Pricing Research (Researcher) application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Value-anchor row cites ≥3 customer interviews (hours saved / revenue added). Operator-estimated anchor is rejected.
rule.rationale: Operator estimates inflate value; customer-reported anchors stay honest.

rule.statement: Van Westendorp requires ≥30 valid responses before the recommended band is published.
rule.rationale: Low-n surveys produce overlapping bands that defeat the survey purpose.

rule.statement: Each tier carries an explicit feature delta. Quota-only tiers require usage-based billing justification.
Final price MUST be validated against ≥5 real paying customers at that price.
rule.rationale: Stated WTP over-estimates by 30–50%; sales correct it.

rule.statement: Recommendation carries a confidence band (low / medium / high) keyed to evidence count (interviews + survey n + sales validation).
rule.rationale: Without confidence bands stakeholders default to high-confidence reading.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
